---
title: "Welcome to my blog"
description: "A first post to show how blog posts work in Quarto."
author: "Your Name"
date: "2026-04-14"
categories: [news, meta]
---

This is an example blog post. To add a new post, create a new folder under
`posts/` (for example `posts/my-new-post/`) and put an `index.qmd` file
inside it. The blog listing page will pick it up automatically.

## What goes in the YAML?

The fields at the top of this file are what the listing uses:

- `title` — shown on the listing and at the top of the post
- `description` — the short blurb under the title on the listing
- `date` — controls the sort order
- `categories` — shown as tags; also power the sidebar filter

## What goes in the body?

Anything you can write in markdown: headings, lists, links, images,
block quotes, footnotes. If you want code, you can show it in a fenced
block — but remember this template doesn't execute code, so keep chunks
as plain examples rather than things that need to run.

Happy writing!
